message:
test(conformance,e2e): factor JSON-output helpers and tighten healthy-doctor assertion

Continuation of the test-hardening pass — extract repeated boilerplate
for reading JSON out of `br` / `bd` command output into three named
helpers, and add a structured assertion for the healthy-doctor path
that the prior "just succeed" version didn't cover.

tests/conformance.rs:
- Add `assert_command_success(output, label)` that reports BOTH stdout
  and stderr in the panic message. Previously many tests only surfaced
  stderr or nothing at all.
- Add `issue_value_from_show_output(output, label) -> Value` that
  asserts success, extracts the JSON payload via the existing
  `extract_json_payload`, parses, and unwraps the common
  `[0]`-if-array pattern. Previously every show-calling test open-
  coded the array-vs-object disambiguation separately.
- Add `assert_json_output_mentions(output, expected, label)` for the
  common "assert success AND the JSON body contains this substring"
  shape.
- Rewrite `conformance_close_sets_closed_at` to use the new helpers
  — collapses ~15 lines of parse+unwrap+array-check into two calls
  to `issue_value_from_show_output`. Same assertions, less noise.

tests/e2e_workspace_scenarios.rs:
- Add `parse_json_stdout(stdout, context) -> Value` that extracts the
  JSON payload and parses with a panic that includes both the context
  string and the payload on failure (previous spelling buried the
  payload).
- Add `assert_doctor_json_has_healthy_checks(json)` that:
    * Finds the checks array (either via `.checks` key or the
      top-level `.as_array()` shape, so old and new doctor schemas
      are both accepted).
    * Asserts at least one check is reported (catches accidental
      empty-doctor-output regressions).
    * Asserts none of the checks have `status == "error"`
      (previously the tests only asserted exit code 0, so an
      `error`-status check in a non-fatal doctor run would slip
      through).
  Applied at each doctor-calling scenario in the file where the
  intent is "healthy workspace" — scenarios testing expected-error
  paths still use the narrower assertions.

Pure test code; no production changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
